generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time occupancy data from building spaces to enable adaptive control policies. Integrated into BIM model with metadata including tenant IDs and endpoint addresses for real-time data exchange via Eclipse Hono."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures environmental temperature conditions in building zones. Data is transmitted to the DT for real-time monitoring and simulation of thermal performance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance LuminanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors lighting levels in adaptive lighting systems (BOREALIS project). Provides real-time luminance data for reinforcement learning-based control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LuminanceDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks energy consumption across building systems (HVAC, lighting, etc.). Data is used for predictive analytics and optimization of energy-efficient operations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance LuminanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LuminanceSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMeter
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched Building Information Model containing device metadata (sensors, actuators, controllers) and middleware configurations (Hono/Ditto endpoints). Serves as the core semantic backbone for DT deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoMiddleware, DittoPlatform, ModelDrivenEngineeringTool
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time sensor data (occupancy, temperature, luminance, energy) transmitted via Eclipse Hono and processed for DT synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, LuminanceDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoPlatform, UnrealEngine5
]

instance SimulationPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived from BIM and operational data, includes life cycle simulations for energy performance, occupant comfort, and adaptive lighting optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReinforcementLearningEngine, UnrealEngine5
]

instance VisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D representation in Unreal Engine 5 for interactive monitoring of building operations, including operational status and performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5
]

// C11: Enablers
instance HonoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono IoT middleware for real-time data ingestion from sensors and devices. Normalizes and transmits sensor data to the DT via standardized APIs."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance DittoPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto digital twin management platform enabling bidirectional communication between physical and virtual worlds. Manages device lifecycle and synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AutomatedCommissioningService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automates model-to-model and model-to-text transformations to generate runtime artifacts (Hono/Ditto configurations, Unreal Engine scenes) from BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedCommissioningService, SimulationService
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Rich visualization environment for interactive 3D monitoring and simulation of building operations. Supports real-time data visualization and life cycle simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, SimulationService
]

instance ReinforcementLearningEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BOREALIS project's reinforcement learning engine for optimizing energy consumption, visual comfort, and non-visual health effects in adaptive lighting systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive 3D visualization of building operations in Unreal Engine 5, including operational status, performance metrics, and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringDashboardInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrates reinforcement learning to optimize energy consumption, visual comfort, and adaptive lighting control policies based on real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationRecommendationInsight, LightingControlPolicyInsight
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables automated deployment of control logic (e.g., lighting semantics) directly from BIM models, reducing manual effort in system configuration."
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomatedLightingCommissioningAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports life cycle simulations (energy performance, occupant comfort) to evaluate design and operational decisions throughout the building's lifecycle."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyPerformanceSimulationInsight, OccupantComfortSimulationInsight
]

instance CustomServiceIntegration : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular architecture with standardized extension points for adding third-party services (IoT analytics, AI-driven maintenance) to the DT ecosystem."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThirdPartyServiceIntegrationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeMonitoringDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Facility Managers receive visualized operational status, performance metrics, and anomaly detection alerts in Unreal Engine 5 dashboards."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Actionable recommendations for operational adjustments to minimize energy consumption based on reinforcement learning predictions."
]

instance LightingControlPolicyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized control policies for adaptive lighting systems derived from real-time luminance and occupancy data."
]

instance AutomatedLightingCommissioningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated deployment of lighting control semantics from BIM models to physical systems, reducing manual configuration effort."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyPerformanceSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Life cycle simulation results for energy performance, informing design and refurbishment decisions."
]

instance OccupantComfortSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-based insights into occupant comfort (thermal, visual) to guide adaptive management strategies."
]

instance ThirdPartyServiceIntegrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic integration of third-party services (e.g., IoT analytics, predictive maintenance) via standardized extension points."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Adaptive lighting fixtures controlled via reinforcement learning policies (BOREALIS project). Receives control signals from the DT to adjust intensity and schedules dynamically."
]

instance HvacActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system actuators (dampers, pumps) that execute control commands from the DT to optimize energy consumption and thermal comfort."
]

instance BlindActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motorized blinds controlled by the DT to regulate daylight and solar heat gain, integrated with lighting and HVAC systems for holistic optimization."
]

// C1: Twinning Process and DT Evolution
instance ModelingPhase : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Phase where Control Engineers enrich BIM models with device metadata (sensors, actuators, controllers) and middleware configurations (Hono/Ditto endpoints)."
]

instance ValidationPhase : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Phase ensuring BIM model consistency and completeness before automated deployment."
]

instance AutomatedDeploymentPhase : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Model-driven transformations generate runtime artifacts (Hono/Ditto configs, Unreal Engine scenes) with minimal manual intervention."
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationPhase : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data exchange between physical and virtual worlds enabled by Eclipse Hono and Ditto."
]

instance ServiceExtensionPhase : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "DT Engineers extend functionality via standardized extension points, allowing iterative upgrades."
]

instance OverallDigitalTwinEntanglementMetric : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "ODTE metric evaluates DT fidelity and guides evolutionary improvements following Design Science Research methodology."
]

instance DesignScienceResearchMethodology : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Emphasizes prototyping and experimentation in the twinning process, ensuring iterative refinement of the DT."
]

// C5: Multiplicities
instance CentralizedDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BimModel, RealTimeOperationalData, SimulationPerformanceData
    base:desc "Single DT instance representing the entire building, managed via unified BIM model and middleware (Hono/Ditto)."
]

instance DecentralizedLightingSubTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LightingActuator, LuminanceSensor, LightingControlPolicyInsight
    base:desc "Sub-twin for lighting systems operating independently or in coordination with the central twin, enabled by standardized extension points."
]

instance DecentralizedHvacSubTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains HvacActuator, TemperatureSensor, EnergyOptimizationRecommendationInsight
    base:desc "Sub-twin for HVAC systems with modular deployment capabilities, supporting hybrid centralized-decentralized architectures."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The interactions from the virtual world to the physical world involve **control signals and actuation commands** generated by the DT and transmitted back to the Building Control System (BCS). These commands are derived from **optimization algorithms, predictive analytics, or user-defined control strategies** (e.g., energy-efficient lighting schedules in TwinLight or adaptive policies in BOREALIS). The DT uses **Eclipse Ditto** to push control signals to physical actuators (e.g., dimming lights, adjusting HVAC settings) via middleware integration. The framework supports **bidirectional communication**, ensuring that virtual adjustments are executed in the physical system while maintaining synchronization through continuous feedback loops."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time (sub-second to millisecond latency)** for critical operational data (e.g., sensor readings, control signals) and **near-real-time (seconds to minutes)** for higher-level analytics and optimization tasks. The framework leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for low-latency data exchange, ensuring synchronization between the physical and virtual worlds. For example, the **BOREALIS project** uses iterative reinforcement learning, which operates on a **time scale of minutes to hours** to refine control policies based on post-occupancy data. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** is used to evaluate the fidelity of synchronization over time."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
    - **BIM Model**: Central semantic repository for building data.
    - **Hono/Ditto Middleware**: Handles real-time data exchange and digital twin management.
    - **Unreal Engine Visualization Layer**: Provides immersive monitoring interfaces.
    - **Extension Points**: Standardized interfaces for integrating custom services (e.g., IoT analytics, AI-driven maintenance).
    - **Control Logic Layer**: Embedded within BIM models (e.g., lighting control semantics in TwinLight) or dynamically generated via reinforcement learning (BOREALIS).
    The constellation supports **scalable deployment** by allowing DTs to be composed of independent sub-systems (e.g., lighting, HVAC) that can be managed centrally or decentralized."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across **all lifecycle stages** of the building, including:
    1. **Design phase**: Leverages BIM models to incorporate static and dynamic data (e.g., geometric, spatial, and control semantics).
    2. **Construction phase**: Supports clash detection, simulation, and documentation generation.
    3. **Operation phase**: Enables real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and adaptive control (e.g., lighting optimization in BOREALIS).
    4. **Refurbishment/Adaptation phase**: Facilitates performance analysis and decision-making for retrofitting or repurposing buildings.
    The DT covers representations of the physical counterpart as **as-designed (ideal)**, **as-manufactured**, and **as-operated**, with continuous synchronization to ensure accuracy. The framework ensures **longevity and compatibility** with AECO standards throughout the lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
    - **Real-time synchronization**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto provide **low-latency data exchange**, minimizing drift between physical and virtual representations.
    - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**: Quantifies the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart, guiding validation efforts.
    - **Model validation**: BIM models are validated for consistency before deployment.
    - **Uncertainty management**: The framework acknowledges potential **semantic drift** (as discussed in Abbasi et al.) and supports **runtime configuration management** to adapt to changes.
    - **Verification and validation mechanisms**: Include **automated testing of model transformations** and **manual reviews by Building Control Engineers** to ensure accuracy."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is established using:
    - **Eclipse Hono**: Acts as an **IoT middleware** for ingesting sensor data and routing it to the DT via standardized protocols (e.g., MQTT, AMQP).
    - **Eclipse Ditto**: Provides **digital twin management** and **bidirectional communication** through a **things-based API**, enabling real-time synchronization.
    - **Network protocols**: The framework supports **industrial IoT protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) for seamless integration with Building Automation Systems (BAS).
    - **Middleware metadata**: Extracted from BIM models to configure **tenant IDs, device IDs, and endpoint addresses** for secure and efficient data transmission."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted and deployed using:
    - **Cloud or edge computing**: The framework supports **scalable deployment** of DT services, with middleware (Hono/Ditto) potentially running on **edge devices** for low-latency processing.
    - **Unreal Engine 5**: Hosts the **visualization layer**, which can be deployed on **high-performance workstations or cloud-based rendering platforms**.
    - **Modular architecture**: Enables **service-oriented deployment**, where components (e.g., analytics, control logic) can be hosted independently and integrated via standardized APIs.
    - **Automated deployment pipeline**: Generated from BIM models via **model-driven transformations**, reducing manual setup and ensuring reproducibility."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is achieved through:
    - **Standardized extension points**: Allow integration with **external information systems** (e.g., ERP, CMMS, IoT platforms) via APIs.
    - **Middleware interoperability**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto support **cross-platform communication** with other DTs or legacy systems.
    - **BIM as a semantic backbone**: Enables integration with **AECO-specific tools** (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) and **industry standards** (e.g., IFC, COBie).
    - **Data normalization**: Sensor data and control signals are standardized before transmission, ensuring compatibility with diverse systems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to:
    - **BIM standards**: Leverages **Industry Foundation Classes (IFC)** and **COBie** for semantic interoperability.
    - **IoT and middleware standards**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto comply with **OASIS standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) for data exchange.
    - **Model-driven engineering (MDE) standards**: Uses **metamodels and domain-specific languages (DSLs)** to ensure consistency in model transformations.
    - **AECO-specific standards**: Aligns with **building automation and control standards** (e.g., ASHRAE, ISO 16750) for operational data."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
    - **Middleware security**: Eclipse Hono and Ditto provide **authentication, authorization, and encryption** for data transmission.
    - **Access control**: Role-based access (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers) ensures **secure interaction** with the DT.
    - **Safety protocols**: The framework supports **remote operation safeguards**, such as **fail-safes and validation checks**, to prevent unintended physical actions (e.g., unsafe lighting adjustments).
    - **Compliance with regulations**: Aligns with **cybersecurity standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001) and **safety protocols** for building automation systems (e.g., IEC 62368-1). The **BOREALIS project** emphasizes **non-visual health effects** (e.g., circadian lighting), ensuring safe and ergonomic operational adjustments."
]
